name: Cucumber Buttermilk Kichadi with Foxtail Millet
source_note: Resources/Recipes/Dinner/Cucumber Buttermilk Kichadi with Foxtail Millet.md
description: The dish uses South Indian evening-tiffin logic: quick-cooking grains, legumes, and aromatic tempering for depth without richness. Cucumber Buttermilk Kichadi with Foxtail Millet brings a distinct structure, seasoning pattern, and grain-legume balance to the table.
cooking_steps: Combine the grain, moong dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce to low heat and cook covered for 18 to 20 minutes, stirring twice, until the mixture turns soft and porridge-like.
Heat oil in a small pan, add cumin, pepper, and ginger, and cook on medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir into the kanji.
Cool for 2 minutes, then fold in the buttermilk for a softer finish before serving.
meal_prep_notes: Kanji keeps 2 days chilled and thickens substantially. Reheat with extra hot water over low heat, then adjust seasoning. For batch cooking, add buttermilk only to the portion being served.
